assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements for foreign exchange risk on an intragroup dividend-related receivable.
The analysis is limited to identifying IFRS hedge accounting approach categories potentially relevant to that foreign exchange exposure.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge accounting as a distinct accounting model for recognised exposures, which is relevant when foreign exchange risk on a recognised monetary position is being considered.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats hedging of highly probable forecast transactions as a separate hedge accounting model, including certain qualifying intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedging a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate top-level hedge accounting model for foreign exchange risk in consolidated financial statements.
